query: My age column has values ranging from 0 all the way up to 250. Should I just drop the outliers before modeling?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend an immediate auto-drop of the outliers; flags this as a decision that requires semantic context first
Requests semantic context for the suspect values — is 0 a missing-value sentinel? is 250 a data-entry error, a years-vs-months confusion, or a legitimate domain extreme?
Recommends contacting the data producer (or otherwise confirming the column's intended encoding) before dropping or imputing
